meta.name: aegis
meta.title: AI Governance Proof-Gap Rubric
meta.id_scheme: Control IDs are AEGIS-prefixed and are NOT NIST subcategory numbers. The real NIST AI RMF subcategory each control maps to is in the `nist_rmf` field.
meta.crosswalk_status: Adversarially reviewed by Codex (GPT-5.5) on 2026-06-02. This is NOT a certified or 'passed' crosswalk — known imprecisions remain, especially exact NIST subcategory and ISO clause numbers. Tags are indicative navigation aids, not legal advice. EU obligations are conditional on the system's legal classification (see AEGIS-GOV-03). Validate NIST subcategories against the NIST AI RMF Core and ISO wording against a licensed ISO/IEC 42001:2023 copy before relying on them.

scoring.rule: VERIFIED_FILE = an inspected artifact substantively implements the control. ATTESTED = no file evidence, but the user gave a concrete specific answer (weaker than file evidence, scored lower). PARTIAL = some but not all evidence. GAP = no evidence and no concrete attestation; absence of evidence is a GAP, never a free pass. NA = control out of scope for this system's classification — EXCLUDED from the denominator entirely, not scored zero.
scoring.weights: VERIFIED_FILE = 1.0, ATTESTED = 0.75, PARTIAL = 0.5, GAP = 0. NA = removed from the denominator (not a zero).
scoring.severity_scale: 1 = good-practice, 2 = important, 3 = critical/blocking for an audit

controls.title: Named accountability owner for the AI system
controls.what_it_checks: A specific person or role owns the outcomes and risks of the AI system.
controls.interview_question: Who is the single named accountable owner (person or role) for this AI system's outcomes and risks?
controls.remediation: Name an accountable owner in GOVERNANCE.md or CODEOWNERS.
controls.applicability: All AI systems (governance hygiene). EU Art. 17 applies to providers of high-risk AI.

controls.nist_rmf: GOVERN 1.2 (characteristics of trustworthy AI integrated into policies)
controls.title: AI acceptable-use / governance policy documented
controls.what_it_checks: A written policy governs how the AI system may and may not be used.
controls.interview_question: Is there a written acceptable-use / AI governance policy for this system? Where does it live?
controls.remediation: Publish an AI acceptable-use policy describing permitted and prohibited uses.

crosswalk.eu_ai_act: Art. 4 AI literacy; Art. 26 deployer obligations

controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 26 applies to deployers of high-risk AI.

controls.nist_rmf: GOVERN 1.1 (legal & regulatory requirements understood, managed, documented)
controls.title: Regulatory applicability register (classification first)
controls.what_it_checks: The system's legal classification is determined and recorded: prohibited (Art. 5) / high-risk (Art. 6 + Annex III) / subject to transparency obligations (Art. 50) / otherwise; whether it is or uses a GPAI model (and whether that GPAI has systemic risk, Art. 51); and the org's role (provider vs deployer, Art. 25). Every EU obligation below is conditional on this.
controls.interview_question: Has someone determined this system's EU AI Act status (prohibited / high-risk / transparency-obligation / other; is it or does it use a GPAI model, and does that GPAI have systemic risk?) and whether you are provider or deployer? Where is that recorded?
controls.remediation: Create a regulatory applicability register classifying the system and your role before relying on any specific obligation.

crosswalk.iso42001: 4.1 context of the organization; 6.1.2 AI risk assessment
crosswalk.eu_ai_act: Arts. 2 (scope), 5 (prohibited practices), 6 + Annex III (high-risk), 50 (transparency obligations), 25 (value-chain roles), 51 (classification of GPAI models with systemic risk)

controls.applicability: All AI systems in scope of the EU AI Act. This control gates the applicability of every other EU tag.

controls.title: Risk-tolerance / risk-management statement
controls.what_it_checks: Documented risk appetite plus a risk register or threat model for the AI system.
controls.interview_question: Is there a documented risk-tolerance statement and a risk register / threat model for this system?
controls.remediation: Document risk tolerance and maintain a risk register or threat model.

crosswalk.iso42001: 6.1.2 AI risk assessment; 6.1.3 AI risk treatment

controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 9 applies to high-risk AI.

controls.nist_rmf: GOVERN 2.2 (training to enable responsible AI roles)
controls.title: Roles, responsibilities & AI competence/training
controls.what_it_checks: People interacting with the system have defined roles and adequate AI literacy/training.
controls.interview_question: Are roles/responsibilities defined and is there AI-literacy training for the people operating this system?
controls.remediation: Define roles and provide AI-literacy training; record it in CONTRIBUTING or an onboarding doc.
controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 4 applies to providers and deployers.

controls.nist_rmf: GOVERN 6.1 (third-party risk policies) — an inventory is a prerequisite for this control, not the whole of it
controls.title: Third-party model & supply-chain inventory
controls.what_it_checks: Base models, datasets, and third-party AI dependencies are inventoried.
controls.interview_question: Do you maintain an inventory of third-party models, datasets, and AI vendors this system depends on?
controls.remediation: Maintain an AI supply-chain inventory (SBOM/model registry) listing base models, datasets, and vendors.

crosswalk.eu_ai_act: Art. 25 responsibilities along the value chain (Art. 53 when GPAI-provider duties apply)

controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 25 applies along the high-risk value chain; Art. 53 only to GPAI providers.

controls.nist_rmf: GOVERN 1.5 (ongoing monitoring & periodic review of the RM process)
controls.title: Periodic governance review cadence
controls.what_it_checks: Governance, risks, and controls are reviewed on a recurring schedule.
controls.interview_question: On what cadence are this system's governance, risks, and controls formally reviewed?
controls.remediation: Set and record a recurring governance review cadence.

crosswalk.eu_ai_act: Art. 17 quality-management system (Art. 72 only for post-market monitoring)

controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 17 applies to providers of high-risk AI.

controls.title: System / model card present
controls.what_it_checks: A model or system card documents what the system is, how it was built, and how it behaves.
controls.interview_question: Is there a model/system card documenting architecture, training/data, and behavior?
controls.remediation: Publish a model/system card (architecture, data, evals, limitations).

crosswalk.iso42001: A.6.2.3 / A.6.2.7 system documentation & technical docs; A.8.2 documentation of AI system

controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 11/Annex IV applies to high-risk AI.

controls.nist_rmf: MAP 1.1 (intended purpose, context of use, requirements)
controls.title: Intended use & limitations documented
controls.what_it_checks: The system's intended use, out-of-scope uses, and known limitations are written down.
controls.interview_question: Where are the intended use, out-of-scope uses, and known limitations documented?
controls.remediation: Document intended use, out-of-scope uses, and limitations in the README or card.
controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 13 applies to high-risk AI.

controls.nist_rmf: MAP 2.2 (documentation of AI system knowledge & limits) — note: data governance is anchored mainly in ISO 42001 A.7 and EU AI Act Art. 10, not a dedicated NIST subcategory
controls.title: Data lineage / provenance & consent
controls.what_it_checks: Training/operational data sources, provenance, and consent/licensing are documented.
controls.interview_question: Can you trace the provenance, consent, and licensing of the data this system uses?
controls.remediation: Document data lineage, consent, and licensing in a data card / datasheet.

crosswalk.iso42001: A.7.2–A.7.5 data for AI (esp. A.7.5 data provenance)
crosswalk.eu_ai_act: Art. 10 data governance (high-risk); Art. 53(1)(c) copyright policy for GPAI providers

controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 10 applies to high-risk; Art. 53 to GPAI providers. GDPR may also apply to personal data.

controls.nist_rmf: MAP 5.1 (likelihood & magnitude of impacts; affected individuals/communities)
controls.title: Stakeholder & impact identification (FRIA where applicable)
controls.what_it_checks: Affected stakeholders and potential impacts (incl. fundamental rights) are identified.
controls.interview_question: Have you identified affected stakeholders and assessed potential impacts (including on rights)?
controls.remediation: Run and document a stakeholder/impact assessment (DPIA/FRIA as applicable).
controls.applicability: All AI systems should assess impact. EU Art. 27 (FRIA) applies only to specified deployers of high-risk AI (raise to severity 3 when it applies).

controls.title: Foreseeable-misuse / abuse analysis
controls.what_it_checks: Reasonably foreseeable misuse and abuse cases are analyzed.
controls.interview_question: Have you analyzed reasonably foreseeable misuse and abuse of this system?
controls.remediation: Document foreseeable-misuse/abuse cases and mitigations.

crosswalk.iso42001: 6.1.2 AI risk assessment; A.5.2 impact assessment

controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 9 applies to high-risk AI.

controls.title: Human-oversight checkpoints
controls.what_it_checks: A human can review/approve/override consequential agent actions.
controls.interview_question: Where can a human review, approve, or override consequential actions this system takes?
controls.remediation: Insert human-oversight/approval checkpoints on consequential actions.

crosswalk.iso42001: A.9.2 processes for responsible use of AI systems

controls.applicability: All agentic AI systems. EU Art. 14 applies to high-risk AI.

controls.title: Evaluation / test coverage exists
controls.what_it_checks: The system has an automated eval/test suite.
controls.interview_question: Where is the eval/test suite for this system and what does it cover?
controls.remediation: Add an automated eval/test suite covering core behaviors.
controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 15 applies to high-risk AI.

controls.title: Safety / red-team evaluations
controls.what_it_checks: Adversarial / safety evaluations (jailbreak, prompt-injection, harmful-output) are run.
controls.interview_question: Do you run safety / red-team evaluations (jailbreak, prompt-injection, harmful output)?
controls.remediation: Add safety/red-team evals (jailbreak, prompt-injection, harmful-content).

crosswalk.eu_ai_act: Art. 15; Art. 55(1)(a) adversarial testing for GPAI with systemic risk

controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 55 applies only to providers of GPAI models with systemic risk.

controls.title: Bias / fairness checks
controls.what_it_checks: Bias and fairness are measured where the system affects people.
controls.interview_question: Do you measure bias/fairness for this system, and how?
controls.remediation: Add bias/fairness measurement appropriate to the use case.

crosswalk.iso42001: A.7.4 quality of data for AI systems

controls.applicability: AI systems affecting people. EU Art. 10 applies to high-risk AI.

controls.title: Logging & observability
controls.what_it_checks: System decisions/actions are logged with enough fidelity to investigate after the fact.
controls.interview_question: What gets logged when the system acts, and can you reconstruct an incident from logs?
controls.remediation: Add structured logging / tracing for model and agent actions (e.g. OpenTelemetry, Langfuse).

crosswalk.iso42001: A.6.2.8 AI system recording of event logs; A.6.2.6 operation & monitoring

controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 12 applies to high-risk AI.

controls.title: Agent tool-permission scoping (least privilege)
controls.what_it_checks: Agents are granted least-privilege, explicitly scoped tool/data access.
controls.interview_question: How is the agent's tool/data access scoped — is it least-privilege and explicitly allowlisted?
controls.remediation: Explicitly allowlist agent tools/data access (least privilege).

crosswalk.iso42001: A.6.2.6 operation & monitoring; A.9.2 responsible use

controls.applicability: All agentic AI systems. EU Art. 15 applies to high-risk AI.

controls.title: Security & resilience testing
controls.what_it_checks: The system is tested for security and resilience beyond tool scoping (adversarial inputs, data poisoning, model extraction, dependency CVEs).
controls.interview_question: How is this system tested for security and resilience (adversarial inputs, poisoning, extraction, dependency CVEs)?
controls.remediation: Add security/resilience testing appropriate to the threat model.
controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 15 applies to high-risk AI.

controls.title: Privacy-risk assessment
controls.what_it_checks: Privacy risks are assessed where the system processes personal data (incl. a DPIA where required).
controls.interview_question: Have you assessed privacy risk (and run a DPIA where personal data and high risk apply)?
controls.remediation: Run a privacy-risk assessment / DPIA where personal data is processed.

crosswalk.iso42001: A.7 data for AI; privacy controls
crosswalk.eu_ai_act: Art. 10 (data); GDPR Art. 35 DPIA where applicable

controls.applicability: AI systems processing personal data. GDPR DPIA where high risk to individuals.

controls.nist_rmf: MEASURE 3.3 (feedback from external stakeholders / affected users)
controls.title: Recourse / appeal / feedback mechanism
controls.what_it_checks: Affected users can report problems, appeal, or get an explanation.
controls.interview_question: How can an affected user report a problem, appeal a decision, or get an explanation?
controls.remediation: Provide a feedback/appeal/recourse channel for affected users.

crosswalk.iso42001: A.8.3 external reporting; A.8.5 information to interested parties
crosswalk.eu_ai_act: Art. 85 (right to lodge a complaint with a market-surveillance authority); Art. 86 (right to explanation of individual decision-making)

controls.applicability: AI systems affecting people. EU Art. 85 (complaints) is broad; Art. 86 (explanation) applies narrowly to specified Annex III high-risk decisions affecting a person. Raise severity when they apply.

controls.nist_rmf: MEASURE 2.4 (deployed system monitored in production — drift)
controls.title: Production monitoring / drift detection
controls.what_it_checks: The system is monitored in production for failures, drift, and anomalies.
controls.interview_question: How is this system monitored in production (failures, drift, anomalies, alerting)?
controls.remediation: Add production monitoring and drift/anomaly alerting.
controls.applicability: All deployed AI systems. EU Art. 72 applies to providers of high-risk AI.

controls.title: Risk treatment, residual risk & deployment go/no-go
controls.what_it_checks: Identified risks have treatment decisions, a named acceptance authority, documented residual risk, and an explicit deployment go/no-go decision. A risk register alone is insufficient.
controls.interview_question: For the top risks, who decided the treatment, who accepted residual risk, and was there an explicit go/no-go before deployment?
controls.remediation: Record treatment decisions, residual risk, an acceptance authority, and a go/no-go gate before deployment.
controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 9 applies to high-risk AI.

controls.nist_rmf: MANAGE 2.4 (mechanisms to supersede, disengage, or deactivate)
controls.title: Rollback / kill-switch capability
controls.what_it_checks: The system can be stopped or rolled back quickly when it misbehaves.
controls.interview_question: How do you stop or roll back this system fast if it starts causing harm?
controls.remediation: Add a kill switch / feature flag and a tested rollback procedure.
controls.applicability: All agentic AI systems. EU Art. 14 applies to high-risk AI.

controls.title: Change management & versioning of models/prompts
controls.what_it_checks: Models, prompts, and config are version-controlled with a change record and review.
controls.interview_question: How are model, prompt, and config changes versioned and reviewed before they ship?
controls.remediation: Version prompts/models and keep a CHANGELOG; gate changes through review.

crosswalk.eu_ai_act: Art. 17(1)(a) QMS; Art. 11 / Annex IV (keep technical docs current)

controls.applicability: All AI systems. EU Art. 17 applies to providers of high-risk AI.

controls.title: AI incident-response plan exists (and is tested)
controls.what_it_checks: A documented, owned, tested plan for what to do when the AI system fails or misbehaves.
controls.interview_question: Is there a tested AI incident-response plan (kill switch, rollback, escalation) and has it been tabletop-tested?
controls.remediation: Create and tabletop-test an AI incident-response plan (this skill generates a starter playbook).

crosswalk.iso42001: A.8.4 communication of incidents; 10.2 nonconformity & corrective action
crosswalk.eu_ai_act: Art. 17(1)(i) internal incident handling (within QMS); Art. 73 serious-incident reporting

controls.applicability: All agentic AI systems. EU Art. 73 reporting applies to providers of high-risk AI.
